creator: _jkrow
caption:
Welp, that’s something I thought I’d never do 😅

Add riding the Olympic skeleton in Lake Placid to your Upstate NY winter bucket list immediately!

And yes, that’s the real Olympic track and an active Olympic training site at Mt Van Hoevenberg 

I had NO idea that regular people could just do this but it’s now safe to say that throwing ourselves head first down an ice chute was hands down one of the most unique experiences I’ve had in New York State so far. 

Our final speeds were 31-35mph, which feels a LOT faster when your face is 3 inches from the ice lol. It gives you a whole new appreciation for the Olympic athletes who reach 80mph+ on this thing 🤯 

WHAT TO KNOW:

💰 Cost is $75 per ride

❄️ It’s weather dependent and can be cancelled if the weather is bad, so I’d recommend NOT leaving it until the last minute of your trip. That way you have time to reschedule if needed

🗓️ Make a reservation online in advance. It’s currently running on weekends in Feb 2026 and the first Sunday in March

🥾 Wear comfy outdoor clothes and gloves (!!)

🏅 This is the real Olympic track, but you’ll start toward the bottom and only do about 1/4 of it and reach speeds of about 35mph. The whole thing is about a mile though, where the athletes reach 80+mph

Are you brave enough to try this experience? Or would you prefer to watch instead? 
——-

👉 The Olympic Skeleton Experience is part of my 3-day Lake Placid Winter Olympic Itinerary. Say ‘Lake Placid’ and I’ll send it over!

transcription.text: That was crazy. We just got to Mount Van Heuvelenburg and we're waiting for a shuttle to ride the skeleton, which is one of the only places in the world where you can actually do this as like a non-Olympic athlete. All right, let's see it. How was it? Awesome. At a speed of 31 miles per hour. That's crazy, I'm like shaking. If you love adrenaline and winter sports, you're going to love that.
